From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Page version status

This is an accepted version of this page

This is the latest accepted revision, reviewed on 3 May 2026.

This article's factual accuracy may be compromised due to out-of-date information. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information. (January 2026)

This is a list of media franchises that have grossed more than $2 billion.

List

[edit]
This is a dynamic list and may never be able to satisfy particular standards for completeness. You can help by editing the page to add missing items, with references to reliable sources.
franchises
Franchise Year of
inception 		Total revenue (est. US$) Revenue breakdown
(est. US$) 		Original medium Creator(s) Owner(s)

$50 billion+

[edit]
Pokémon 1996 $115 billion[a]
  • Retail sales – $103.6 billion[b]
  • Mobile games – $10.23 billion[c]
  • Box office – $1.16 billion[29]
  • Home video – $40 million[d]
  • Jet aircraft livery sales – $3 million[e]

(Nintendo, Creatures, Game Freak)
(copyright; trademark in Japan)
Nintendo (trademark globally)
Mickey Mouse & Friends 1928 $61.2 billion
  • Retail sales – $60.66 billion[f]
  • Box office – $226.7 million[g]
  • VHS & DVD – $323 million[h]

Ub Iwerks 		The Walt Disney Company
Winnie-the-Pooh (original books and Disney franchise) 1924 (original books)
1966 (Disney franchise) 		$50.2 billion

  • Box office – $545 million[58]

Walt Disney Animation Studios (Disney franchise) 		The Walt Disney Company

    • The Pokémon Company no longer mentions how much the franchise has earned on their website, as of May 2019[update]

    • Over ¥6 trillion ($55 billion) revenue up until March 2017[2]

    • Until 2005 – $25 billion[3][4][5]
